behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: A pure unit test of the validator can miss real failures in CSV parsing-to-validation-to-DLQ wiring (for example, row-number offset, header handling, or serialization mismatch). This crosses module boundaries and storage/queue adapters.
behaviors.test_description: Run the ingestion path end-to-end in-process with a real CSV parser and real DLQ sink implementation (local test queue/table). Feed a CSV containing known missing required fields at specific line numbers (including around header/blank-line edges). Assert invalid rows are absent from normalized output and present in DLQ with exact original row numbers.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic transformation logic at a function/object boundary. No I/O or external system is needed to catch parsing/normalization failures if the parser is directly exercised with representative inputs.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit tests for the date normalization function with valid ISO 8601, US (MM/DD/YYYY), and European (DD/MM/YYYY) cases, plus ambiguity boundaries. Assert canonical output format and deterministic handling rules for ambiguous inputs.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: This depends on runtime memory behavior, GC, process limits, and wall-clock performance under realistic load. In-process mocks do not expose true OS/process resource failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline as a real process against a generated 500,000-row CSV and real configured sinks in a controlled test environment. Enforce the production timeout threshold, capture peak RSS/heap, and fail if process exceeds memory budget, is OOM-killed, or exceeds timeout.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: Retry correctness here is at the HTTP/client-runtime boundary (status handling, retry middleware, timing/backoff behavior). Mocking the S3 client can hide real SDK/network behavior and make false passes.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against an HTTP endpoint (S3-compatible stub) that returns 503 for the first N requests, then succeeds. Measure request timestamps and verify exponential backoff windows and eventual successful batch completion. Assert no premature batch failure.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes durable DB state plus real restart/crash semantics. Integration tests in one process can miss checkpoint/transaction behavior that only appears across process restarts.
behaviors.test_description: Start pipeline as a subprocess with real PostgreSQL. Force termination mid-batch after a known subset is committed, then restart with same input/checkpoint state. Assert final table contains exactly one copy per logical row (via primary key/unique key counts) and full expected cardinality.
